calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($1,731,917 USD / 304,014 total beneficiaries) = $5.70 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 176 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

metrics.context_qualifier: Life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries for street dogs and cats in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 4

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs and cats receiving critical medical care, rehabilitation, and comfort in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: This year, we treated 22,736 dogs and cats—an increase of 6%, delivering critical medical care, rehabilitation, and comfort to animals in need.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 7

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals receiving life-saving care through community outreach teams in 2024.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 13

metrics.context_qualifier: Life-saving operations performed by veterinary surgeons in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: Behind the scenes, our veterinary surgeons performed 1,038 life-saving operations, while our front office staff managed 17,714 calls for help—a 4% rise over the previous year.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 7

metrics.context_qualifier: Total residents, patients, and foster animals under Soi Dog's care by the end of 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: By the close of the year [2024], 1,877 animals were under the care of Soi Dog, which includes residents, patients and those in temporary foster care.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 8

metrics.evidence_quote: A total of 461 rescued animals – 308 dogs and 153 cats – left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 16

metrics.evidence_quote: 43.599 tonnes of food provided for animals affected by the floods
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 11

metrics.evidence_quote: The team also engaged 1,885 children through educational sessions and events, inspiring them to become compassionate advocates for animals.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 13

metrics.context_qualifier: Investigations launched by the social change team to tackle animal cruelty in 2024.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 13

metrics.evidence_quote: they successfully rehabilitated 78 dogs, helping them to overcome past trauma, learn to trust, and develop the confidence needed to thrive in homes.
metrics.source_citation: Soi Dog End of Year Report 2024, p. 14

significant_events.source_quote: In 2024 alone, we neutered a record 267,100 animals. After two decades of relentless effort, Soi Dog now operates the world's largest CNVR (Catch, Neuter, Vaccinate and Return) programme.
significant_events.summary: Soi Dog operates the world's largest CNVR programme, neutering a record 267,100 animals in 2024.

significant_events.event_name: Soi Dog Becomes Legal Entity in Vietnam & Dog Meat Trade Advocacy
significant_events.source_quote: Our biggest milestone came in November, when Soi Dog Foundation officially became a legal entity in Vietnam after receiving an operating registration certificate, achieved in a record-breaking time frame of just seven months. With a new office, local staff, and deeper community integration, we're proving that lasting change comes from within.
significant_events.summary: Soi Dog Foundation officially became a legal entity in Vietnam, leading to a city-wide directive against dog/cat meat consumption in Hanoi.

significant_events.source_quote: Alongside our trusted partners, Animal Kingdom Foundation, we rescued 24 dogs from the meat trade, including Basti (pictured above), who is now thriving in his loving home. His transformation is a powerful reminder of what is possible when cruelty ends and compassion begins. We also helped secure 10 arrests and 2 convictions of dog meat traders, sending a strong message: animal cruelty will not go unpunished.
significant_events.summary: Rescued 24 dogs from the meat trade and secured 10 arrests and 2 convictions of dog meat traders in the Philippines.

significant_events.source_quote: This momentum carried through to November, when Bangkok became one of the first cities to join the Positive Cities Pledge—a global commitment to humane and sustainable stray dog population management.
significant_events.summary: Bangkok became one of the first cities to join the Positive Cities Pledge for humane stray dog population management.

significant_events.source_quote: In September, we proudly launched our 19th mobile CNVR clinic, joining two others already stationed in Surat Thani, Thailand's sixth-largest province.
significant_events.summary: Launched the 19th mobile CNVR clinic in September, expanding capacity to improve lives of street animals across Thailand.

significant_events.event_name: Emergency Flood Response and Aid Delivery
significant_events.source_quote: When unprecedented flooding struck, we acted without hesitation, delivering over 43 tonnes of food to animals in crisis—all thanks to your unwavering support.
significant_events.summary: Delivered over 43 tonnes of food to animals affected by severe flooding in northern and southern Thailand.

significant_events.source_quote: Guided by our expert teams, 62 veterinary students gained hands-on CNVR experience, while another 96 embraced the opportunity to delve into shelter medicine through our exclusive course in partnership with the Faculty of Veterinary Medicine at Chulalongkorn University.
significant_events.summary: 62 vet students gained hands-on CNVR experience and 96 delved into shelter medicine through university partnerships.

significant_events.event_name: Animal Welfare Education in Phuket Schools
significant_events.source_quote: We formalised a partnership with the Phuket Primary Education Office, integrating animal welfare into the curriculum of eight local schools.
significant_events.summary: Formalized partnership with Phuket Primary Education Office, integrating animal welfare into 8 local schools' curriculum.

significant_events.event_name: Adoption of Calzone by Thai Royal Family
significant_events.source_quote: August brought one of the year's most memorable moments: the adoption of a Soi Dog into none other than the Thai royal family.
significant_events.summary: Calzone, a rescued dog, was adopted by Her Royal Highness Princess Aditayadornkitikhun, a significant moment for animal welfare.

data.original_text: Soi Dog's CNVR programme – the largest of its kind in the world today - is targeted at reducing the number of animals born into a life of suffering and abuse or neglect, eliminating rabies and stopping the spread of other infectious and deadly diseases among stray dogs and cats.

As part of a wider community engagement initiative, Soi Dog runs humane education, community liaison and outreach programmes targeted to help improve the welfare of both stray and domestic animals, educating local schoolchildren and the wider public on animal welfare whilst providing support to network of volunteer 'feeders', caring members of the local community who act as guardians for stray animals.

Soi Dog campaigns for an improvement in animal welfare rights across Asia and was the driving force behind the promulgation of Thailand's first-ever animal welfare act introduced in 2014, bringing in new laws specifically covering cruelty to animals.
The Soi Dog Foundation Gill Dalley Sanctuary in Phuket welcomes visitors and volunteers from all over the world, year-round.
For animals unable to be returned safely to the streets, Soi Dog provides sanctuary - a haven where sick, injured and vulnerable dogs and cats receive world-class medical care, and love.

Soi Dog oversees local, national and international adoptions, directly rehoming thousands of dogs and cats rescued from dire situations across the region, from government shelters, and from the horrendous dog and cat meat trade.
Soi Dog Foundation USA supports and promotes the animal welfare efforts of Soi Dog Foundation, a legally registered charitable organization based in Phuket, Thailand.

Soi Dog Foundation mission statement: Improve the welfare of dogs and cats in Asia, resulting in better lives for both the animal and human communities, to create a society without homeless animals and to ultimately end animal cruelty.
Target demographics: Support and promote the efforts of Soi Dog Foundation in Southeast Asia.

Direct beneficiaries per year: Neuter and vaccinate almost 200,000 dogs and cats, provide veterinary treatment to over 17,000 animals, deliver hundreds of tons of food and urgent supplies to tens of thousands of stricken animals affected by natural and man-made disasters, save dogs and cats from the meat trade in the Philippines.
The majority of Soi Dog's work is focused on an holistic approach including Rescue, Sterilization, Vaccination, Medical Treatment, Shelter and Adoption.
Community Outreach and Education Outreach are also integral parts of their programs.

Soi Dog's teams of animal rescue officers, coordinated by a hospital office team, travel the island of Phuket and nearby province of Phang Nga to respond to animal emergencies and reports of stray dogs or cats requiring hospital treatment.
In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.
A total of 461 rescued animals – 308 dogs and 153 cats – left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.
Soi Dog Foundation has almost eradicated rabies from Thailand.
Phuket in particular has been declared rabies free – such is the power of vaccination.
By the close of the year [2024], 1,877 animals were under the care of Soi Dog, which includes residents, patients and those in temporary foster care.
